 {"id":"bd-22vu","title":"[IDEA-17.3] E2E/CI vector regen + diff","description":"## Scenario
- Regenerate vectors in CI and diff against repo.
- Fail with a clear message on drift.

## Logging
- Emit a JSON diff summary as CI artifact.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Persist JSONL artifacts and validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl`.

 {"id":"bd-23d7","title":"[IDEA-21.1] Implement zone policy diff + rollback CLI","description":"## What
Implement `fcp policy diff` and `fcp policy rollback --plan`.

## How
- Load two policy objects by ObjectId.
- Produce a structured diff (added/removed/changed fields).
- Emit rollback plan object (not executed automatically).

## Success Criteria
- CLI outputs both human and JSON diffs.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- CLI returns JSON diff with `added/removed/changed` sections.
- Rollback plan includes previous policy ObjectId.

-{"id":"bd-24bc","title":"Policy Bundles: Signed ZonePolicy Packs + Diff/Preview/Rollback","description":"# Policy Bundles: Signed ZonePolicy Packs + Diff/Preview/Rollback

## Goal
Provide a safe, versioned workflow to propose, review, preview, apply, and roll back policy changes across zones.

## Why (User Impact)
- Prevents accidental policy regressions and privilege creep.
- Enables deterministic review with clear diffs and risk summaries.
- Makes policy changes auditable and reversible with one command.

## Scope
- PolicyBundle object (signed, versioned, immutable).
- Deterministic diff and risk summary engine.
- Preview evaluator (would-allow/deny deltas).
- CLI workflow for create/diff/preview/apply/rollback.

## Non-goals
- UI/editor; CLI only for now.

## Deliverables
- Canonical schema + signature rules for PolicyBundle.
- Diff + risk summary outputs with stable ordering.
- Preview evaluation reports with reason codes.
- CLI commands with JSON/human outputs.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E script with structured logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy bundles are signed and verifiable; invalid signatures fail closed.
- Preview explains allow/deny deltas before apply.
- Rollback to previous bundle is one command and auditable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:28.006280446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:23:59.892522101Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","policy","security"]}

Likely reuse these patterns for PolicyBundle diff/risk engine and CLI workflow once schema work (bd-2f42) stabilizes. Next: pick bd-c0fv (diff/risk) or bd-1dh7 (preview) and implement on top of existing policy types, keeping stable ordering + reason codes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"WhiteDesert","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:28.006280446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T05:05:47.168274732Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","policy","security"]}

 {"id":"bd-24j6","title":"[TRACE] MeshNode capture hooks","description":"# [TRACE] MeshNode capture hooks

## Goal
Capture FCPC/FCPS frames and decisions from MeshNode with sampling and size caps.

## Deliverables
- Capture hooks for routing/admission/lease decisions.
- Configurable sampling, size limits, and on/off controls per zone.
- Trace output written as mesh objects or local files for replay.

## Tests
- Unit tests for capture inclusion/exclusion.
- Integration test for trace completeness in a small mesh scenario.
- Structured JSON logs for capture decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Capture overhead is bounded and configurable.
- Traces conform to schema and redact sensitive fields.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:33.060487711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:27:43.448710167Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24j6","depends_on_id":"bd-21fq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:06:21.108278940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24j6","depends_on_id":"bd-3tja","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:05:33.073056095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24j6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:06:26.015221220Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-254d","title":"[IDEA-14] Connector Limits Constants Generator","description":"# Connector Limits Constants Generator

## Goal
Standardize platform-specific limits (message length, embed caps, payload sizes) via a generated `limits.rs` module per connector to avoid magic numbers.

## Background / Why
Discord and Telegram both require strict length limits; scattered constants are easy to miss. A canonical `limits.rs` improves correctness and testability.

## Scope
- Generator support in `fcp new` and documentation.
- Optional lint to ensure connectors define limits constants.

## Deliverables
- `limits.rs` template with TODO placeholders and defaults.
- Guidance on how to update limits when APIs change.

## Tests
- Unit test in generator to ensure limits file generated.
- Connector test helper verifying limit constants used in validation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New connectors have a `limits.rs` by default.
- Validation functions use limits constants, not magic numbers.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:08.279393213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:55.514131459Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-254d","depends_on_id":"bd-35ct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:55.514069805Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9k4e","title":"TEST: Rate Limit Declaration Unit/Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Rate Limit Declaration Unit/Integration Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive test coverage for SDK rate limit declarations.

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod rate_limit_unit_tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_declaration_complete() {
        // Every tool maps to at least one pool
        // Every referenced pool exists
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_serialization_roundtrip() {
        // Declarations serialize/deserialize correctly
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_pool_validation() {
        // Reject invalid configs (zero window, negative requests)
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_scope_semantics() {
        // Instance, Credential, Global scopes are distinct
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_enforcement_levels() {
        // Hard, Soft, Advisory behave differently
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_rate_limit_unit_types() {
        // Requests, Tokens, Bytes, Custom units
    }
}
```

## Integration Tests

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_host_aggregates_rate_limits() {
    let harness = HostTestHarness::new().await;
    harness.install_connector("test-ratelimited").await.unwrap();

    let aggregated = harness.get_aggregated_rate_limits().await.unwrap();
    assert!(aggregated.limits.iter().any(|l| l.pool.id == "test_api"));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_introspect_includes_rate_limits() {
    let harness = CliTestHarness::new().await;
    harness.install_connector("test-ratelimited").await.unwrap();

    let result = harness.run_command(&["connector", "introspect", "test-ratelimited", "--json"]).await.unwrap();
    let tools: Vec<ToolDescriptor> = serde_json::from_str(&result.stdout).unwrap();

    for tool in &tools {
        if let Some(limits) = &tool.rate_limits {
            assert!(!limits.is_empty(), "Tool {} declares rate limits but list is empty", tool.name);
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All rate limit types serialize correctly
- [ ] Host aggregates limits from multiple connectors
- [ ] CLI introspect shows rate limit info
- [ ] Invalid rate limit configs are rejected at compile time where possible","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:38.054501609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:48:59.273009917Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:48:59.272960534Z","close_reason":"Added rate limit declaration validation + unit tests; fixed SDK test import","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","sdk","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9k4e","depends_on_id":"bd-2rnf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-c0fv","title":"[POL] Diff + risk summary engine","description":"# [POL] Diff + risk summary engine

## Goal
Provide deterministic diffs and risk summaries between policy bundles.

## Deliverables
- Diff engine (stable ordering) for:
  - Zone ceilings, capability grants, roles, and resource bindings.
  - Network constraints expansions (hosts/ports/CIDR changes).
  - Approval/elevation/declassification rules.
- Risk summary with reason codes and severity tiers.

## Design Notes
- Output must be stable and canonical for reproducible reviews.
- Risk summary flags widening egress, new risky caps, and lowered integrity.

## Tests
- Unit tests for diff correctness and ordering stability.
- Golden snapshot tests for representative bundle pairs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Same inputs always yield identical diff output.
- Summary flags risky changes with clear reason codes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:50.698990530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:24:24.331371522Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c0fv","depends_on_id":"bd-24bc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:00:50.706306644Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-c0fv","depends_on_id":"bd-2f42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:01.512440918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-c0fv","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:02:05.530722436Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-c4cm","title":"[IDEA-26] Source Diversity Enforcement + Metrics","description":"## Background
Spec requires source diversity to avoid single-source reconstruction. We need mechanical enforcement and metrics to prove diversity SLOs.

## Goals
- Track per-object symbol source diversity.
- Enforce minimum distinct sources for reconstruction when policy requires.
- Emit metrics and structured logs for coverage and diversity.

## Non-goals
- Full repair controller redesign (only diversity enforcement + metrics).

## Approach
- Extend symbol store metadata to track source node IDs.
- Add policy flag for `min_source_diversity` per zone.
- Integrate with repair loop to fetch from additional sources.

## Risks
- Extra network cost; ensure policy-gated and bounded.

## Success Criteria
- Single-source prevention triggers with stable reason codes.
- Metrics expose diversity coverage per object.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Single‑source reconstruction is denied when policy requires diversity.
- Diversity metrics are surfaced for each object.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"TurquoiseBear","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:47:34.314512457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:38:41.892635645Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:38:41.892536902Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
